---
title: "Code text"
---

The coding screen has three main areas:

- codebook on the left
- source text in the centre
- applied codes in the right-side inspector

![](../assets/guide/coding-view-with-codebook.png)

## Read the source map

The source map is the small grid below the codebook.

Each square is one source in the project. The current source is highlighted. Sources with coding activity show their status in the grid, so you can quickly see where you are in the dataset.

Use the source map as a progress check, not as a detailed report. For detailed review, use [Review coded text](audit.qmd) or export the annotations.

## Navigate sources

Use the source grid to choose a source.

Within a source:

- press `Up` or `Down` to move sentence by sentence
- press `Shift+Left` or `Shift+Right` to move between sources
- click text when you want to select manually

## Apply codes

Steps:

1. Select the passage you want to code.
2. Apply a code from the codebook.
3. Check the highlight and applied-code summary.

You can apply visible codes with keyboard shortcuts. The codebook shows the available shortcut beside each code.

If the same code is applied to overlapping text, ACE merges the overlapping spans into a single annotation for that code.

## Review applied codes

The right-side applied-codes inspector shows codes already used in the current source. Use it to check whether a source has been coded at all and whether the code mix looks plausible.

To remove a code, use the delete control for that applied code.

If a source has no codes yet, the panel says so.

## Undo and redo

Use undo and redo while coding to recover from recent changes.

Undo history is kept for the open project during the current app session.

## Recommended rhythm

1. Move to the next sentence.
2. Read it.
3. Select the exact passage if needed.
4. Apply one or more codes.
5. Check the highlight.
6. Move on.
